w:r.w:t: US and Europe / SEPA - Reporting Module Roadmap Inventory

This document covers payment operations, payment statistics, payment execution, settlement, reconciliation, SLA, availability, and scheme/operator compliance reports only. Fraud, SAR, AML, sanctions, OFAC, suspicious-activity, and financial-crime reports are intentionally excluded
A. US - Mandatory / Regulatory / Scheme Payment Reports
Key Data Attributes Required from Payment Engine
Continuous evidence; produced on request during Federal Reserve / examiner review

payment ID, message ID, sender FI, receiver FI, debtor account, creditor account, amount, currency, payment received timestamp, settlement timestamp, account posting timestamp, funds availability timestamp, customer notification timestamp, delay flag, delay duration, delay reason, final payment status
Continuous monitoring; monthly internal; produced on request

Service date, uptime percentage, planned downtime, unplanned downtime, degraded service window, start time, end time, duration, payments received, payments sent, payments delayed, payments rejected, payments pending, value impacted, recovery timestamp, reason for downtime, affected system component
Continuous operational record; produced on request

Payment ID, message ID, message type, sender FI, receiver FI, amount, currency, initiation timestamp, receipt timestamp, settlement timestamp, posting timestamp, payment status, accepted/rejected indicator, rejection reason, return request status, response timestamp, customer account posting reference
Settlement and Master Account Posting Reconciliation Report
Daily internal reconciliation; produced on request during audit/examination
Federal Reserve Banks / bank examiner / internal and external audit

transaction ID, settlement timestamp, sender master account, receiver master account, debit posting, credit posting, customer account posting, settlement finality timestamp, reconciliation status, unmatched item flag, suspense item flag, adjustment reference, resolution timestamp
Participant Limit / Risk Control Breach Report

Participant ID, sender FI, receiver FI, payment ID, transaction amount, participant send limit, participant receive limit, customer transaction limit, account limit, limit breach timestamp, rejected due to limit yes/no, limit override flag, configuration version, effective date of limit
Return / Request for Return Processing Report
Event-based; daily/monthly operational evidence; produced on request

payment ID, original message ID, return request ID, return reason, original amount, returned amount, sender FI, receiver FI, request timestamp, response timestamp, accepted/rejected return status, final settlement status, customer account adjustment reference
Continuous validation; produced on request / release compliance review

Message ID, message type, ISO message version, business application header, payment ID, instructed amount, settlement amount, debtor details, creditor details, debtor agent, creditor agent, mandatory field completeness, validation errors, rejection code, repair status, final processing status
Fedwire Funds Transfer Message Audit Report
Continuous recordkeeping; produced on request during examination/audit
Federal Reserve Banks under Regulation J and Operating Circular 6

Fedwire message ID, IMAD, OMAD, sender ABA, receiver ABA, business date, amount, currency, payment type, input timestamp, processing timestamp, settlement timestamp, sender reference, originator details, beneficiary details, message status, reject code, operator/user/system ID, approval trail
Fedwire ISO 20022 Message Format Compliance Report
Continuous validation; produced on request / release compliance review
Federal Reserve Banks / Fedwire Funds Service

message version, business application header, payment ID, UETR if applicable, instructed amount, interbank settlement amount, debtor, creditor, debtor agent, creditor agent, mandatory field completeness, validation errors, rejected messages, repaired messages, structured remittance data
Daily operational evidence; produced on request
Federal Reserve Banks / Fedwire Funds Service

Payment ID, Fedwire business date, input timestamp, settlement timestamp, value date, cut-off category, accepted before cut-off yes/no, late payment flag, payment status, rejection reason, operator action, approval timestamp, manual override flag
Fedwire Settlement Finality / Posting Reconciliation Report
Daily internal reconciliation; produced on request
Federal Reserve Banks / bank examiner / internal and external audit

Fedwire reference, IMAD, OMAD, sender ABA, receiver ABA, debit account, credit account, amount, currency, settlement timestamp, posting reference, GL reference, reconciliation status, unmatched item, duplicate item, suspense posting, adjustment reference, resolution date
Fedwire Payment Rejection / Return Operational Report
Daily/monthly operational evidence; produced on request
Federal Reserve Banks / Fedwire Funds Service

Original payment ID, IMAD, OMAD, sender ABA, receiver ABA, amount, rejection timestamp, return timestamp, reject/return reason code, rejection category, message validation issue, account issue, cut-off issue, operational issue, final status, resubmission status
US Payment Service Availability Impact Report
Event-driven; produced during service disruption / regulatory review
OCC / Federal Reserve / FDIC depending on bank; also relevant to
, Fedwire, ACH, internal payments if affected

Incident ID, affected rail, affected payment service, downtime start, downtime end, planned/unplanned flag, payments delayed, payments failed, payments rejected, payments pending, value impacted, customer impact, recovery timestamp, manual processing used, reconciliation completed yes/no
US Computer-Security Incident - Payment Impact Report
Event-driven; notification required no later than 36 hours after determination of a qualifying notification incident
OCC / Federal Reserve / FDIC depending on primary regulator
, Fedwire, ACH, internal payments, other payment channels affected

Incident ID, determination timestamp, affected payment rail, payment initiation impact, clearing impact, settlement impact, posting impact, number of payments delayed, number of payments failed, number of payments rejected, value impacted, customer impact, recovery timestamp, manual processing used, reconciliation status
Continuous evidence; produced on request during performance/SLA review
Federal Reserve Banks / bank examiner / internal audit

Payment ID, rail, channel, amount, currency, initiation timestamp, validation timestamp, clearing submission timestamp, settlement timestamp, posting timestamp, customer notification timestamp, total processing time, internal processing time, external clearing time, SLA status, breach reason
Cross-US Ancillary Service Latency Report for Payment Execution
Continuous evidence; produced on request during performance/outage review
Federal Reserve Banks / bank examiner / internal audit

Payment ID, rail, validation response time, account status check time, balance check time, limit check time, core posting response time, clearing adapter response time, notification service response time, timeout flag, failed service, retry count, overall payment SLA impact
Cross-US Settlement and Reconciliation Break Report
Federal Reserve Banks / bank examiner / internal/external audit

Payment ID, rail, clearing reference, settlement reference, debit account, credit account, settlement amount, settlement timestamp, posting status, GL reference, matched/unmatched flag, reconciliation break type, suspense account reference, ageing, resolution owner, resolution timestamp
Daily / continuous monitoring; produced on request
Federal Reserve Banks / treasury/risk examiner / payment-system operator

Opening liquidity balance, available balance, reserved balance, payment outflows, payment inflows, failed payments due to liquidity, participant limit, customer limit, transaction limit, breach timestamp, payment ID, amount, currency, top-up timestamp, top-up amount
Federal Reserve Banks / bank examiner / payment-system operator

Payment ID, rail, amount, currency, rejection timestamp, rejection code, rejection category, validation failure, format failure, account failure, limit failure, timeout, liquidity failure, technical issue, customer segment, resubmission status, final status
Key Data Attributes Required from Payment Engine
Periodic; generally quarterly / semi-annual depending on National Central Bank collection model
National Central Banks / National Competent Authorities
SCT, SCT Inst, SDD Core, SDD B2B, card payments, e-money payments, account-to-account payments

Reporting PSP/BIC, legal entity, country, payment instrument, scheme, direction, transaction count, transaction value, currency, domestic/cross-border flag, debtor country, creditor country, customer type, channel, initiation mode, execution date, settlement date, payment status, reject/return count, payment account type
SEPA Credit Transfer Volume & Value Report
Periodic; usually part of ECB/NCB payment statistics submission

Payment ID, instruction ID, end-to-end ID, debtor account, creditor account, debtor PSP, creditor PSP, amount, currency, initiation date, execution date, settlement date, domestic/cross-border flag, customer segment, channel, accepted count, rejected count, returned count, total value
SEPA Instant Credit Transfer Volume & Value Report
Periodic; part of ECB/NCB statistics and instant-payment oversight
ECB / National Central Bank / National Competent Authority

Payment ID, transaction ID, end-to-end ID, amount, currency, debtor PSP, creditor PSP, received timestamp, accepted timestamp, settlement timestamp, beneficiary credit timestamp, domestic/cross-border flag, transaction count, total value, accepted count, rejected count, timeout count, 24x7 processing indicator
SEPA Direct Debit Core Volume & Value Report
Periodic; usually part of ECB/NCB payment statistics

Mandate ID, creditor ID, debtor account, creditor account, collection amount, currency, collection date, settlement date, mandate type, first/recurring/final/one-off sequence, successful collections, rejects, returns, refunds, reversals, domestic/cross-border flag, total count, total value
SEPA Direct Debit B2B Volume & Value Report
Periodic; usually part of ECB/NCB payment statistics

Mandate ID, creditor ID, debtor PSP, creditor PSP, debtor account, creditor account, collection amount, settlement date, sequence type, mandate validation status, successful collections, rejects, returns, reversals, domestic/cross-border flag, total count, total value
EU Instant Payments Regulation - Credit Transfer Charges Report
National Competent Authority; EBA uniform reporting framework

PSP identifier, country, customer segment, payment account type, standard credit transfer fee, instant credit transfer fee, channel, package/bundle indicator, fee currency, fee waiver, domestic/cross-border indicator, fee comparison between SCT and SCT Inst, reporting period
EU Instant Payments Regulation - Payment Account Charges Report
National Competent Authority; EBA uniform reporting framework
Payment accounts used for SCT / SCT Inst

Account type, customer segment, monthly account fee, annual account fee, payment package, included transfer limits, additional transaction fee, fee currency, account plan, domestic/cross-border applicability, reporting period
EU Instant Payments Regulation - Rejection Share Report
National Competent Authority; EBA uniform reporting framework
SCT Inst and relevant credit transfers

Total initiated transactions, total rejected transactions, rejection share percentage, national transaction rejection share, cross-border transaction rejection share, rejection reason category, reject timestamp, payment amount, currency, debtor PSP, creditor PSP, customer segment, reporting period
SCT Inst 10-Second Execution SLA Evidence Report
Continuous evidence; produced ad hoc for regulator/scheme audit or incident review
EPC / National Competent Authority / payment-system overseer

Payment ID, received timestamp, validation completion timestamp, clearing submission timestamp, settlement timestamp, confirmation received timestamp, beneficiary credit timestamp, customer notification timestamp, total elapsed time, within 10 seconds yes/no, timeout yes/no, timeout reason, delayed component, CSM/clearing system, debtor PSP, creditor PSP
SCT Inst Timeout / Reservation Release Report
Event-driven; maintained continuously and produced on request

Payment ID, original amount, payer account, reserved amount, reservation timestamp, timeout timestamp, reservation release timestamp, reason for timeout, whether customer balance was restored, final payment status, reversal/retry status, customer notification timestamp
SCT Inst Availability / 24x7 Service Continuity Report
Continuous evidence; monthly internal control; produced on request
EPC / National Competent Authority / payment-system overseer

Service availability percentage, planned downtime, unplanned downtime, start time, end time, duration, affected payment services, payments delayed, payments rejected, payments timed out, value impacted, backlog count, recovery timestamp, failover status, customer impact
Daily/monthly operational evidence; produced on request for scheme/regulatory review
SCT, SCT Inst, SDD Core, SDD B2B

Original transaction ID, original end-to-end ID, original amount, R-transaction type, reject/return/recall/refund/refusal/reversal indicator, ISO reason code, EPC reason code, R-message timestamp, original settlement date, elapsed days, debtor PSP, creditor PSP, customer segment, final status
SEPA Recall / Request for Recall by Originator Report
Event-driven; retained and produced on request

Original payment ID, recall request ID, recall reason, request timestamp, debtor PSP, creditor PSP, original amount, recovered amount, rejected recall reason, acceptance/rejection status, response timestamp, final closure status
Daily internally; produced on request to regulator/scheme/auditor
National Central Bank / scheme overseer / internal or external audit
SCT, SCT Inst, SDD Core, SDD B2B

Payment ID, clearing reference, settlement reference, settlement account, settlement date, settlement cycle, debit amount, credit amount, currency, posting status, matched/unmatched status, reconciliation break type, suspense account reference, ageing, resolution timestamp
SEPA Liquidity / Prefunding Position Report for Instant Payments
Daily/internal continuous monitoring; produced on request
Central Bank / payment-system operator / scheme overseer
SCT Inst via TIPS, RT1 or other CSMs

Opening liquidity balance, available balance, reserved balance, payment outflows, payment inflows, failed payments due to liquidity, liquidity top-up amount, top-up timestamp, threshold breach, participant limit, settlement account, currency, reporting date
DORA Major ICT Incident - Payment Impact Report
Event-driven: initial notification, intermediate report, final report
All payment services affected: SCT, SCT Inst, SDD, internal payments, API payments

Incident ID, start time, detection time, classification time, recovery time, affected payment rails, affected services, number of delayed payments, number of failed payments, number of rejected payments, number of duplicated payments, value impacted, customer impact, SLA impact, backlog count, recovery action, reconciliation status
DORA Payment Service Availability Impact Report
Event-driven as part of major ICT incident reporting

Service name, downtime start, downtime end, planned/unplanned flag, availability percentage, customer channels affected, payment initiation affected yes/no, clearing affected yes/no, settlement affected yes/no, posting affected yes/no, number/value of impacted transactions, recovery timestamp
Evidence report; produced on request during instant-payment/scheme/regulatory review
National Competent Authority / payment-system overseer / EPC

Payment ID, rail, channel, amount, currency, initiation timestamp, validation timestamp, clearing submission timestamp, settlement timestamp, posting timestamp, notification timestamp, total processing time, internal processing time, external clearing time, SLA status, breach reason
Cross-SEPA Ancillary Service Latency Report for Payment Execution
Evidence report; produced on request during SLA breach/outage review

Payment ID, rail, validation service response time, account status check time, core posting time, liquidity check time, clearing adapter response time, notification service time, timeout flag, failed service, retry count, overall SLA impact
Periodic internal; mandatory evidence where rejection-share or scheme review applies
National Competent Authority / EBA for IPR rejection-share / EPC
SCT, SCT Inst, SDD Core, SDD B2B

Payment ID, rail, amount, currency, reject timestamp, reject code, reject category, validation failure, account failure, limit failure, format failure, timeout, liquidity failure, customer segment, debtor PSP, creditor PSP, national/cross-border flag, resubmission status
Cross-SEPA Settlement and Reconciliation Break Report
National Central Bank / scheme overseer / internal and external audit
SCT, SCT Inst, SDD Core, SDD B2B

Payment ID, rail, clearing reference, settlement reference, settlement amount, settlement currency, settlement cycle, debit posting, credit posting, GL reference, matched/unmatched flag, reconciliation break type, suspense account, ageing, resolution owner, resolution timestamp
Europe / SEPA Payment-Only Regulatory, Scheme and Evidence Reports

This catalogue expands each Europe/SEPA payment report into an individual report specification. The objective is to help define the reporting-module roadmap, the data mart design, the reusable payment evidence layer and the minimum data fields needed from the payment engine.

Important product interpretation: Not every item is a fixed calendar submission. Some are statutory recurring reports, while others are mandatory scheme/regulatory evidence that must be produced during audit, incident review, operational resilience review, or regulator/scheme query.
Payment-only regulatory / scheme / audit evidence report
Mandatory statistical reporting under ECB/NCB payment statistics framework.
Quarterly / semi-annual, depending on national collection
ECB through National Central Banks / NCAs
SCT, SCT Inst, SDD Core, SDD B2B, cards, e-money, A2A payments
ECB payment statistics regulation and national central bank payment statistics returns.
Provides regulator-level aggregated visibility of non-cash payment activity processed by the PSP, including count, value, instrument, channel, geography and status dimensions.
Reporting period, Payment rail/scheme, Country / branch / legal entity, Currency, Direction, Customer segment, Channel, Status, Domestic vs cross-border
Regulator-ready aggregate table, Transaction-level drilldown, Exception list, CSV/XLSX export, PDF evidence pack, Audit trail of generation time/user/template version
Scheme-specific filters: SCT, SCT Inst, SDD Core, SDD B2B, cards, e-money, A2A payments
Summary KPIs, trend by period, exception drilldown, transaction grid, exportable report package
Provides the regulator with an aggregated view of non-cash payment activity processed by the PSP: volume, value, instruments, geography, channels and payment outcomes.
Prepared for the statutory reporting cycle; transaction-level evidence should be available continuously so the bank can reconcile submitted aggregates to source payments.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module

The payment engine must supply certified payment-level and aggregate data to the regulatory reporting platform. It should support period-end extracts, restatements, audit trails and reconciliation to settlement/posting systems.
by reporting period, payment instrument, scheme, direction, country, channel and customer segment
Tie totals to final payment lifecycle status and settlement/posting evidence.
Payment ID, instruction ID, end-to-end ID, transaction ID, clearing reference, original transaction reference where applicable.
Without stable identifiers, aggregation cannot be reconciled to transaction-level evidence and regulator/examiner drilldown will fail.
Debtor account, creditor account, debtor PSP/BIC, creditor PSP/BIC, country, customer segment, channel.
Incorrect party/PSP data distorts domestic/cross-border reporting, customer-segment reporting, and scheme-level accountability.
Instructed amount, settlement amount, currency, debit/credit indicator, fees where applicable.
Errors directly impact volume/value statistics, settlement evidence, customer charges reporting, and reconciliation.
Initiation/receipt, validation completed, clearing submission, settlement, posting/credit, customer notification, final status timestamp.
Prevents inability to prove execution time, value date, settlement timing, SLA compliance, or operational delay root cause.
Accepted, rejected, returned, recalled, timed out, settled, pending; ISO and scheme reason codes.
Regulator cannot understand payment outcomes or rejection shares; operations cannot evidence why a payment failed or was returned.
Reporting period, payment instrument, scheme, direction, domestic/cross-border flag, initiation channel, customer type, payment account type.
Wrong dimensions lead to inaccurate ECB/NCB returns and difficult restatement when the NCB queries a specific cell.
Provide both summary view and transaction-level drilldown to support audit and regulator queries.
Store report template version, generated timestamp, generated by, data-as-of timestamp and source extract ID.
Support filters by period, rail, direction, country, PSP, channel, customer segment, status and reason code where relevant.
Maintain immutable payment lifecycle history rather than relying only on current status.
Allow export in Excel/CSV and API extract format, with maker-checker approval for final certified extracts.
SEPA Credit Transfer Volume & Value Report
Payment-only regulatory / scheme / audit evidence report
Mandatory where the institution reports credit transfer payment statistics.
Periodic; usually included in ECB/NCB statistics
ECB/NCB payment statistics taxonomy for credit transfers.
Provides the SCT-specific view of credit-transfer activity, allowing the bank to report and evidence how many SCT payments were processed, settled, rejected and returned.
Reporting period, Payment rail/scheme, Country / branch / legal entity, Currency, Direction, Customer segment, Channel, Status, Domestic vs cross-border
Regulator-ready aggregate table, Transaction-level drilldown, Exception list, CSV/XLSX export, PDF evidence pack, Audit trail of generation time/user/template version
Summary KPIs, trend by period, exception drilldown, transaction grid, exportable report package
Gives the regulator and the bank a rail-specific view of standard euro credit transfer activity and execution outcomes.
Generated for periodic statistics cycles and on request when the regulator asks for SCT-specific activity or exception analysis.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The payment engine must distinguish SCT from SCT Inst and non-SEPA credit transfers, identify final status, settlement date and cross-border classification.
Calculate total accepted, settled, rejected and returned SCT transactions.
Aggregate by country, PSP, channel, customer segment and value band.
Provide drilldown from aggregate totals to payment IDs.
Payment ID, instruction ID, end-to-end ID, transaction ID, clearing reference, original transaction reference where applicable.
Without stable identifiers, aggregation cannot be reconciled to transaction-level evidence and regulator/examiner drilldown will fail.
Debtor account, creditor account, debtor PSP/BIC, creditor PSP/BIC, country, customer segment, channel.
Incorrect party/PSP data distorts domestic/cross-border reporting, customer-segment reporting, and scheme-level accountability.
Instructed amount, settlement amount, currency, debit/credit indicator, fees where applicable.
Errors directly impact volume/value statistics, settlement evidence, customer charges reporting, and reconciliation.
Initiation/receipt, validation completed, clearing submission, settlement, posting/credit, customer notification, final status timestamp.
Prevents inability to prove execution time, value date, settlement timing, SLA compliance, or operational delay root cause.
Accepted, rejected, returned, recalled, timed out, settled, pending; ISO and scheme reason codes.
Regulator cannot understand payment outcomes or rejection shares; operations cannot evidence why a payment failed or was returned.
Scheme code, service level, local instrument, payment type, batch/file indicator.
If classification is weak, SCT volumes may be mixed with instant, internal or cross-border/wire payments, creating regulatory misreporting.
Provide both summary view and transaction-level drilldown to support audit and regulator queries.
Store report template version, generated timestamp, generated by, data-as-of timestamp and source extract ID.
Support filters by period, rail, direction, country, PSP, channel, customer segment, status and reason code where relevant.
Maintain immutable payment lifecycle history rather than relying only on current status.
Allow export in Excel/CSV and API extract format, with maker-checker approval for final certified extracts.
SEPA Instant Credit Transfer Volume & Value Report
Payment-only regulatory / scheme / audit evidence report
Mandatory statistics/evidence for instant-payment operation and reporting.
Periodic; statistics plus instant-payment oversight evidence
ECB payment statistics and Instant Payments Regulation oversight needs.
Provides the SCT Inst-specific activity view, including count, value, acceptance, rejection, timeout and 24x7 processing evidence
Reporting period, Payment rail/scheme, Country / branch / legal entity, Currency, Direction, Customer segment, Channel, Status, Domestic vs cross-border
Regulator-ready aggregate table, Transaction-level drilldown, Exception list, CSV/XLSX export, PDF evidence pack, Audit trail of generation time/user/template version
Summary KPIs, trend by period, exception drilldown, transaction grid, exportable report package
Shows adoption, usage and successful execution of instant payments, including rejected and timed-out instant payments.
Prepared for periodic payment statistics and for ad hoc instant-payment supervision or scheme review.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The engine must capture millisecond-level timestamps and final instant payment outcome, not just a generic credit-transfer status.
Aggregate initiated, accepted, settled, rejected and timed-out SCT Inst transactions.
Track 24x7 processing and timestamp chain for instant execution evidence.
Payment ID, instruction ID, end-to-end ID, transaction ID, clearing reference, original transaction reference where applicable.
Without stable identifiers, aggregation cannot be reconciled to transaction-level evidence and regulator/examiner drilldown will fail.
Debtor account, creditor account, debtor PSP/BIC, creditor PSP/BIC, country, customer segment, channel.
Incorrect party/PSP data distorts domestic/cross-border reporting, customer-segment reporting, and scheme-level accountability.
Instructed amount, settlement amount, currency, debit/credit indicator, fees where applicable.
Errors directly impact volume/value statistics, settlement evidence, customer charges reporting, and reconciliation.
Received, validation completed, clearing sent, settlement confirmation, beneficiary credit, notification timestamps with millisecond precision where available.
Without precise timestamps, the bank cannot evidence instant execution, timeout handling or SLA root cause.
Accepted, rejected, returned, recalled, timed out, settled, pending; ISO and scheme reason codes.
Regulator cannot understand payment outcomes or rejection shares; operations cannot evidence why a payment failed or was returned.
Within instant window, timed out, reservation released, accepted after timeout, beneficiary credited.
Missing outcome flags prevent accurate instant payment oversight and customer-impact analysis.
Provide both summary view and transaction-level drilldown to support audit and regulator queries.
Store report template version, generated timestamp, generated by, data-as-of timestamp and source extract ID.
Support filters by period, rail, direction, country, PSP, channel, customer segment, status and reason code where relevant.
Maintain immutable payment lifecycle history rather than relying only on current status.
Allow export in Excel/CSV and API extract format, with maker-checker approval for final certified extracts.
SEPA Direct Debit Core Volume & Value Report
Payment-only regulatory / scheme / audit evidence report
Mandatory where the institution reports direct debit payment statistics.
Periodic; usually included in ECB/NCB statistics
ECB/NCB payment statistics taxonomy for direct debits.
Provides SDD Core collection statistics and exception handling evidence across mandate, sequence and collection lifecycle.
Reporting period, Payment rail/scheme, Country / branch / legal entity, Currency, Direction, Customer segment, Channel, Status, Domestic vs cross-border
Regulator-ready aggregate table, Transaction-level drilldown, Exception list, CSV/XLSX export, PDF evidence pack, Audit trail of generation time/user/template version
Summary KPIs, trend by period, exception drilldown, transaction grid, exportable report package
Reports SDD Core collection activity, including successful collections and post-settlement exceptions such as rejects, returns, refunds and reversals.
Prepared for periodic reporting and for scheme or regulator review of direct debit volumes and exception
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The engine must link collections to mandates and sequence types and capture R-transaction events over the allowed lifecycle.
Aggregate collections by collection date, settlement date, creditor, country and sequence type.
Separate first, recurring, one-off and final collections.
Count successful collections and all R-transaction outcomes.
Payment ID, instruction ID, end-to-end ID, transaction ID, clearing reference, original transaction reference where applicable.
Without stable identifiers, aggregation cannot be reconciled to transaction-level evidence and regulator/examiner drilldown will fail.

Errors directly impact volume/value statistics, settlement evidence, customer charges reporting, and reconciliation.
Mandate ID, creditor ID, mandate type, sequence type, signature/activation indicator, debtor/creditor references.
Without mandate linkage, returns/refunds cannot be explained and SDD scheme compliance evidence is weak.
Initiation/receipt, validation completed, clearing submission, settlement, posting/credit, customer notification, final status timestamp.
Prevents inability to prove execution time, value date, settlement timing, SLA compliance, or operational delay root cause.
Accepted, rejected, returned, recalled, timed out, settled, pending; ISO and scheme reason codes.
Regulator cannot understand payment outcomes or rejection shares; operations cannot evidence why a payment failed or was returned.
Debtor PSP, creditor PSP, debtor country, creditor country, domestic/cross-border flag.
Incorrect geography distorts payment statistics and can misstate cross-border direct debit activity.

Support filters by period, rail, direction, country, PSP, channel, customer segment, status and reason code where relevant.
Maintain immutable payment lifecycle history rather than relying only on current status.
Allow export in Excel/CSV and API extract format, with maker-checker approval for final certified extracts.

ECB/NCB payment statistics taxonomy for direct debits.

Reporting period, Payment rail/scheme, Country / branch / legal entity, Currency, Direction, Customer segment, Channel, Status, Domestic vs cross-border
Regulator-ready aggregate table, Transaction-level drilldown, Exception list, CSV/XLSX export, PDF evidence pack, Audit trail of generation time/user/template version

Initiation/receipt, validation completed, clearing submission, settlement, posting/credit, customer notification, final status timestamp.
Prevents inability to prove execution time, value date, settlement timing, SLA compliance, or operational delay root cause.

Payment-only regulatory / scheme / audit evidence report
Mandatory reporting of charges for credit transfers and instant credit transfers.
National Competent Authority; EBA uniform reporting ITS
Instant Payments Regulation / SEPA Regulation Article 15(3) reporting.
Demonstrates that instant credit transfer charges are reportable and comparable against standard credit transfer charges for the same customer/channel/account context.
Reporting period, Payment rail/scheme, Country / branch / legal entity, Currency, Direction, Customer segment, Channel, Status, Domestic vs cross-border
Regulator-ready aggregate table, Transaction-level drilldown, Exception list, CSV/XLSX export, PDF evidence pack, Audit trail of generation time/user/template version
Summary KPIs, trend by period, exception drilldown, transaction grid, exportable report package
Demonstrates that instant credit transfers are not priced above comparable standard credit transfers for the same customer and channel context.
Annual regulatory submission and on request during fee/pricing compliance review.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The payment-reporting module must merge payment-engine volumes with fee/charge configuration and actual charged-fee evidence.
Compare SCT and SCT Inst fees by customer segment, channel, account package and domestic/cross-border category.
Identify fee waivers, bundles and included transaction allowances.
Provide evidence from actual payment charges and tariff master.
Consumer/corporate/SME indicator, payment account type, account package, pricing plan.
Wrong segmentation can make a compliant price look non-compliant or hide excessive charges.
Standard CT fee, instant CT fee, fee currency, fee waiver, bundle indicator, actual charged amount.
Missing fee data prevents proof of equal or lower pricing for instant payments.
SCT/SCT Inst indicator, online/mobile/API/file/branch, domestic/cross-border flag.
Pricing must be compared like-for-like; missing channel/scheme data invalidates comparison.
Reporting period, tariff version, effective date, transaction sample IDs.
Without versioned evidence, annual numbers cannot be defended during review.
Provide both summary view and transaction-level drilldown to support audit and regulator queries.

Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
Must identify settlement/posting breaks by rail, amount, currency, ageing and resolution status.
Compare payment-engine records with clearing/settlement statements and ledger postings.
breaks as unmatched, duplicate, suspense, wrong amount or pending.
Payment ID, instruction ID, end-to-end ID, transaction ID, clearing reference, original transaction reference where applicable.

Clearing reference, settlement reference, debit/credit posting, GL reference.

Matched/unmatched flag, break type, amount difference, suspense account, ageing.

EPC SCT Inst rulebook: SCT Inst enables euro credit transfers with funds made available in less than ten seconds at any time.

initial, intermediate and final incident reporting; draft RTS specify time-limit structure for notification and follow-up reporting.
Common Data Controls Across All Europe / SEPA Payment Reports
Every aggregate cell should trace back to payment IDs and source-system extract IDs.
Regulator queries often ask how a number was produced and whether it reconciles to source transactions.
Maintain report template version, rule version, mapping version and generation timestamp.
Required for restatements, regulatory changes and year-on-year comparability.
Store full lifecycle status changes, not just the current state.
Needed for turnaround time, SLA, exception and incident-impact reporting.
Maintain ISO reason code, EPC reason code and internal reason category mapping.
Avoids inconsistent rejection/return classifications across reports.
Tie payment-engine status to clearing, settlement and ledger posting references.
Ensures statistical reports and financial-control reports do not diverge.
Capture report generation user/system, approval workflow and export history.
Supports certified reporting and prevents uncontrolled regulatory submissions.
. Official Reference Pointers Used for Requirement Framing
ECB payment statistics and National Central Bank payment-statistics collections for non-cash payment instruments, credit transfers and direct debits.
EBA final draft ITS / uniform reporting framework for Instant Payments Regulation reporting on credit-transfer charges, payment-account charges and rejected transactions.
EPC SEPA Credit Transfer and SEPA Instant Credit Transfer scheme rulebooks, including instant execution and R-transaction handling.
DORA major ICT-related incident reporting framework and national competent authority templates for initial, intermediate and final incident reports.
SEPA payment-system operator and scheme oversight expectations for settlement, liquidity, availability, reconciliation and operational evidence.
USA Payment Reports - Individual Detailed Report Catalogue
The USA payment reporting module should focus on payment execution, settlement, posting, availability, reconciliation, message validation, limits, and operational evidence across
, Fedwire, ACH, and internal payment rails.
, reports must capture real-time payment processing, immediate funds availability, 24x7x365 service readiness, return processing, participant limits, and ISO 20022 message validation.
For Fedwire, reports must support message audit, settlement finality, cut-off compliance, ISO 20022 validation, rejection/return tracking, and posting reconciliation.

The module should also provide cross-rail reports for turnaround time, service latency, reconciliation breaks, liquidity/limit breaches, rejection root cause, and payment service availability impact. Fraud, AML, SAR, OFAC, and sanctions-related reports are excluded from this scope.
Payment execution / instant-payment funds availability evidence
participants; not a routine public filing but must be evidenced when examined or challenged.
Continuous capture; daily internal control; produced on request or during incident/service review.
is designed for instant payments with receiver access to funds immediately.
Validates that beneficiary funds were made available immediately after
Business date, participant/FI, direction, sender/receiver, amount range, customer segment, payment status, availability delay flag, delay reason, time bucket.
Transaction drilldown, delayed-payment list, SLA buckets, average/maximum availability delay, exception ageing, participant-wise delay trend, payment timeline view.
Instant-payment trust depends on the receiver being able to use funds immediately; this report proves that the bank did not merely receive the message but posted funds to the customer account.
readiness review, customer dispute, operational incident, degraded service review, internal audit, or regulator/examiner request.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The reporting module must capture message receipt, settlement, posting, funds-availability and customer-notification timestamps in one payment timeline.
Calculate availability delay as funds availability timestamp minus
receipt/credit advice timestamp. Flag exceptions where delay exceeds internal SLA or scheme expectation. Group by participant, account, rail, and reason.
Primary evidence to trace the exact instant-payment message across gateway, clearing, settlement and account posting.
Shows which participant originated and received the payment, useful for counterparty-specific exceptions.

Ensures only successful/settled items are included in availability statistics.

Continuous monitoring; monthly internal control; event-based submission/evidence when requested.

The module must correlate service-monitoring events with payment-processing events to convert technical downtime into payment impact.
Calculate availability as available minutes divided by total minutes in period. Link downtime windows to affected payment IDs using timestamp overlap and affected component mapping.

Provides the headline availability metric expected by operations and oversight teams.
Distinguishes approved maintenance from resilience failure.

Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The reporting module must maintain immutable payment states and timestamped state transitions for every payment.
Build lifecycle from state-event table. Current status is the latest valid state. Ageing equals current timestamp minus latest status timestamp for open/pending payments.
Distinguishes payment, return, request for return, acknowledgement, status or administrative messages.
Provides financial context for each payment.

Mandatory operational evidence and audit control; required during reconciliation, Federal Reserve review or bank examination.
Daily internal reconciliation; produced on request.
settles obligations between participants through entries to Federal Reserve Bank Master Accounts.
settlement entries with bank ledger/customer postings and identifies breaks.
Business date, master account, participant, amount range, break type, matched/unmatched, ageing, resolution status.
Matched/unmatched items, suspense items, ageing buckets, settlement-to-posting drilldown, participant-wise breaks, resolution tracker.

Triggered daily after settlement cycle, during month-end controls, audit, customer dispute, or Federal Reserve/examiner review.

settlement reference, master-account entry, customer posting reference and GL/suspense records.

Participant Limit / Risk Control Breach Report
Payment limit and scheme risk-control report
Mandatory operational evidence for participant risk controls; produced on request or after limit-related rejection.
Continuous monitoring; daily exception review; ad hoc request.
Operating Procedures include participant risk-management controls and interface capabilities to manage limits and related settings.
Shows payments blocked, rejected or delayed due to participant, customer, account or transaction limits.
Date range, participant, customer/account, limit type, breach type, amount range, rejected/allowed, override flag.
chart, rejected-payment drilldown, override audit, top limit-triggering customers/participants.
Limits protect the bank and the scheme from excessive exposure and operational risk; breaches must be explainable and auditable.
Triggered after limit rejection, threshold breach, configuration change review, scheme review or internal risk-control audit.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must store limit configuration versions and apply them to each payment decision to prove why a payment passed or failed.
Compare transaction amount and cumulative exposure against active limits at payment time. Flag payment if amount exceeds limit or usage crosses threshold. Preserve config version used.
Separates monitored breaches from enforced rejections.
Provides audit trail for exceptional processing.
Proves which limit settings were active.
Shows validity of limit configuration at transaction time.
Add configurable thresholds, limit trend analytics, auto-alerts at 80/90/100%
Return / Request for Return Processing Report
participant procedures; produced on request, dispute, audit or scheme review.
Event-based; daily/monthly internal exception reporting; produced on request.
procedures include payment return and request-for-return message handling as part of operational processing.
Tracks original payment, return request, response, settlement and customer adjustment for
Original payment date, return request date, return reason, status, sender/receiver FI, amount range, ageing, recovery status.
Return queue, ageing list, accepted/rejected return drilldown, original-vs-returned amount comparison, closure status.
Instant payments are final once settled, so return processing needs transparent evidence and clear linkage to the original transaction.
Triggered by customer error, beneficiary FI request, operational error, duplicate payment, dispute or return workflow.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must link every return/request-for-return message to the original
Map return request to original payment ID. Calculate response time and returned amount. Determine closure status based on return settlement/customer adjustment.
Links return to the initial settled payment.
Shows full or partial return status.
Identifies counterparties involved in return handling.
Starts ageing clock for response tracking.
Proves customer account correction occurred where applicable.

message processing; produced on request or release validation.
Continuous validation; daily exception review; release/regression reporting.
operates using ISO 20022-based message flows and participant procedures that require valid message formatting and processing.
Identifies message validation failures, missing mandatory fields and repaired/rejected messages.
Message type, business date, participant, validation error, field name, severity, rejected/repaired, release version.
Validation-error list, field-level drilldown, message payload trace, rejection trend, release comparison, repair queue.
Invalid payment messages cause rejects, delays and operational failures; the bank must prove conformance to scheme message requirements.
Triggered during onboarding, release upgrade, ISO schema change, high reject rate, production issue or scheme audit.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must capture schema validation results, business-rule validation results and final payment processing outcome.
Validate each message against active ISO schema and business rules. Store failed rule IDs and map to payment status/rejection reason.
Confirms compatibility with current scheme version.
Proves routing and business context were populated.
Links validation result to business transaction.
Connects validation failure to payment outcome.
Identifies whether issue was corrected or resubmitted.
Add schema-version comparison, automated field-quality dashboard and downloadable invalid-message sample pack.
Fedwire Funds Transfer Message Audit Report
Wire payment message audit / transaction record
Mandatory recordkeeping/evidence for Fedwire participants; produced during Federal Reserve, bank examiner or audit review.
Continuous recordkeeping; daily operational extracts; ad hoc examination/audit evidence.
Fedwire Funds Service is operated by Federal Reserve Banks under Regulation J and Operating Circular 6; payments are processed as immediate, final and irrevocable once accepted.
Provides transaction-level Fedwire message evidence from input through settlement and final status.
Business date, sender ABA, receiver ABA, amount range, payment type, status, operator, reject code, time window.
Wire payment grid, IMAD/OMAD drilldown, message audit trail, status timeline, operator activity, rejection analysis.
Large-value wire transfers require strong auditability, traceability and settlement evidence.
Triggered during wire investigation, customer inquiry, operational dispute, audit, regulator examination or reconciliation break.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must preserve complete Fedwire identifiers, message status transitions, settlement time and user/system audit actions.
Capture each Fedwire transaction event and join with IMAD/OMAD, sender/receiver ABA, status and settlement details. Display latest status and full history.
Primary technical trace for wire message.
Input Message Accountability Data used for Fedwire tracing.
Output Message Accountability Data used for receiver-side tracing.
Distinguishes customer transfer, bank transfer or
Shows final outcome and reason for failure.
Supports audit and non-repudiation of payment actions.

Fedwire ISO 20022 Message Format Compliance Report
Mandatory technical evidence for Fedwire ISO 20022 processing and release compliance.
Continuous validation; daily exception review; release or migration validation.
Federal Reserve Banks publish Fedwire ISO 20022 implementation resources and message requirements for Fedwire participants.
Monitors Fedwire ISO 20022 message completeness, validation errors and data-quality exceptions.
version, business date, sender/receiver, validation error, field name, UETR presence, repair status.
Invalid-message list, mandatory-field defects, message schema version analysis, repaired/rejected trend, field-completeness score.
ISO 20022 migration increases structured data requirements; format defects can cause payment rejects, repair cost and compliance issues.
Triggered during Fedwire ISO release changes, production validation, high repair rate, bank examination or payment-data quality review.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must validate message payloads against Fedwire ISO schema, business rules and internal mandatory-field policy.
Run schema validation and field-completeness checks. Score each payment for mandatory field completeness and classify errors by severity.
Confirms which ISO message definition applies.
Supports payment traceability and cross-border linkage where applicable.
Ensures amount fields are consistent and valid.
Confirms required party and FI information.
Add ISO message data-quality KPI, field-level heatmap, and automated change-impact report for ISO releases.
Fedwire Cut-off / Operating Window Compliance Report
Mandatory operational evidence for Fedwire operating-window controls; produced on request.
Daily operational evidence; ad hoc examination/audit evidence.
Fedwire operates under Federal Reserve operating schedules, Regulation J and Operating Circular 6 procedures.
Proves whether Fedwire payments were submitted, processed and settled within applicable operating windows and cut-offs.
Business date, cut-off category, payment type, status, late flag, sender/receiver, amount range, operator, value date.
Late-payment list, before/after cut-off trend, manual override list, rejected due to timing, operating-day reconciliation.
Cut-off compliance prevents incorrect value dating, operational disputes, failed settlements and customer service issues.
Triggered at day-end, after late payment exception, audit, customer complaint, Fedwire operating review or release test.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must maintain Fedwire calendar/cut-off configuration and compare each payment timestamp against the active schedule.
Determine applicable operating window based on payment type and business date. Flag payments submitted after cut-off or settled on unexpected value date.
Links timing result to specific wire.
Determines whether instruction was submitted before cut-off.
Identifies date intended for payment execution.
Add calendar/cut-off configuration screen, late-payment alerting and day-end exception certification.
Fedwire Settlement Finality / Posting Reconciliation Report
Mandatory operational/audit evidence for Fedwire settlement and posting controls.
Daily internal reconciliation; produced on request.
Fedwire payments are real-time gross settlement payments with finality once processed through Federal Reserve Bank accounts.
Reconciles Fedwire final settlement against customer/core ledger and GL postings.
Business date, sender/receiver ABA, amount range, matched/unmatched, break type, ageing, resolution status, GL account.
Reconciliation dashboard, unmatched wires, duplicate postings, suspense items, GL mismatch drilldown, ageing and owner tracker.
Fedwire finality means mistakes must be detected quickly through reconciliation rather than reversed casually.
Triggered daily after Fedwire processing, during month-end close, audit, customer dispute or operational incident.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must join Fedwire message records, settlement entries, customer postings and GL entries using deterministic match keys.
Match by IMAD/OMAD, amount, business date, sender/receiver and posting reference. Classify breaks and calculate ageing until resolution.
Core reconciliation keys across Fedwire records.
Links payment to internal accounting entries.
Add auto-match confidence scoring, suspense workflow and reconciliation evidence export for auditors
Mandatory operational evidence for failed or returned Fedwire payments; produced on request.
Daily/monthly operational evidence; ad hoc examination/audit evidence.
Fedwire rules and operating procedures require valid wire instructions and status/reject handling under Federal Reserve processing.
Explains why Fedwire payments were rejected, returned or resubmitted.
Business date, reject/return reason, status, sender/receiver, payment type, amount range, resubmission status, operator.
Rejection trend, top reject codes, returned-payment list, resubmitted items, ageing queue, reason-code drilldown.
High rejection levels indicate payment-data quality, format, timing or operational issues that can create regulatory and customer risk.
Triggered for daily exception management, high reject rate, audit review, client escalation or Federal Reserve operational query.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must capture reject/return reason code, classify root cause and link exception to original payment and resubmission.
Group rejected/returned payments by reason code and root-cause category. Link resubmissions using original reference and customer/account match.
Official reason for failure or return.
Shows whether issue remained open or resolved.
Indicates whether payment was corrected and resent.
Add automated reason-code mapping, reject trend analytics, and data-quality improvement backlog generation.
US Payment Service Availability Impact Report
Mandatory evidence during service disruption and payment-system review; not a routine public filing.
Event-driven; monthly internal availability review recommended.
, Fedwire, ACH, internal payments if affected
Banking supervisors and payment-system operators expect banks to evidence operational resilience and customer/payment impact for service disruptions.
Quantifies how a payment service disruption affected payment initiation, clearing, settlement and posting.
Incident ID, affected rail, component, date/time window, severity, planned/unplanned, status, customer segment, recovery status.
Incident payment-impact summary, delayed/failed/rejected list, value impacted, recovery timeline, rail-wise breakdown, backlog ageing.
Technical availability alone is not enough; regulators and operators ask how many payments and customers were actually affected.
/Fedwire outage, core posting issue, file/channel outage, clearing adapter failure, or resilience audit.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must correlate incident management records with payment lifecycle events and quantify count/value impact.
Identify payments whose lifecycle timestamps overlap outage window or affected component. Classify impact as delayed, failed, rejected, pending or manually processed.
Connects payment impact to incident record.
Defines time window for impact calculation.
Add incident-to-payment auto-linking, impact estimator, regulator-ready evidence pack and customer communication export.
US Computer-Security Incident - Payment Impact Report
Regulatory incident notification support / payment impact annex
Mandatory event-driven evidence where a qualifying notification incident affects payment operations.
Event-driven; regulator notification within 36 hours after determination for covered banking organizations.
, Fedwire, ACH, internal payments and other payment channels affected
OCC/Federal Reserve/FDIC Computer-Security Incident Notification rule requires notification no later than 36 hours after determining a notification incident occurred.
Provides the payment-specific annex for a cyber/technology incident notification and follow-up review.
Incident ID, determination timestamp, affected rail, service, impact category, customer segment, recovery status, value threshold.
Payment impact summary, delayed/failed/rejected counts, value impacted, timeline, recovery status, manual processing evidence, reconciliation status.
A cyber or technology incident becomes materially important for a bank when it disrupts payment execution, clearing, settlement or customer access.
Triggered only when the bank determines a qualifying computer-security notification incident and payment operations are impacted.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The reporting module must quickly produce payment counts, values, affected rails, customer impact and recovery position within the regulatory notification window.

From incident time window and affected components, query impacted payments and classify by lifecycle outcome. Calculate count/value and produce current-state summary for notification and final review.
Links payment report to regulatory incident case.
Shows which payment function failed or degraded.
Add one-click incident annex export, current-state snapshot, and automated link to IT incident tool.
Mandatory evidence where scheme/operator/examiner asks for payment performance and SLA proof.
Continuous capture; daily/monthly internal reporting; ad hoc review.
Payment operators and bank examiners can review whether payment processing is timely, reliable and consistent with service expectations.
Measures end-to-end processing time from payment initiation to clearing/settlement/posting/customer notification.
Rail, business date, customer segment, amount range, channel, status, time bucket, breach reason, participant.
Average/median/95th percentile processing time, SLA breach list, payment timeline drilldown, rail-wise performance, bottleneck analysis.
Turnaround time reveals whether the payment engine can support instant and time-critical payments without hidden processing delays.
Triggered during performance review, customer complaint, incident,
readiness, Fedwire operating review or management control.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must capture consistent timestamp milestones across all rails and calculate internal, external and total processing time.
Total TAT equals final posting/notification timestamp minus initiation/receipt timestamp. Component TAT is calculated from adjacent lifecycle events.
Add percentile analytics, bottleneck waterfall chart and proactive SLA breach alerting.
Cross-US Ancillary Service Latency Report for Payment Execution
Payment component latency / dependency performance report
Mandatory evidence during SLA breach, outage, release review or examiner query.
Continuous capture; daily/monthly internal reporting; ad hoc review.
Banks must evidence operational control over payment dependencies that affect timely payment execution and customer posting.
Shows which internal or external service caused payment delay: validation, balance, limit, core posting, clearing adapter or notification.
Rail, service/component, date range, response-time bucket, timeout flag, error code, retry count, payment status, participant.
Component latency trend, timeout list, service-wise SLA breach, payment-to-component drilldown, retry/error analysis, bottleneck heatmap.
Payment SLA failures are often caused by dependent services; this report identifies the actual bottleneck instead of only showing final delay.
Triggered when payments breach SLA, during performance tuning, incident review, vendor review, or release regression.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must log request/response times for every payment-dependency call and correlate them to the payment lifecycle.
For each component call, calculate response time as response timestamp minus request timestamp. Aggregate per payment and mark the longest/failed component as probable delay driver.
Shows which payment rail was affected.
Shows whether component delay caused payment breach.
Add component-level SLA thresholds, service owner mapping, and dependency observability dashboard.
Cross-US Settlement and Reconciliation Break Report
Mandatory internal control and audit evidence; produced on examiner/operator request.
Payment settlement and ledger integrity are core operational controls for banks and are reviewed by audit and banking examiners.
Identifies unmatched, duplicate, suspense and ageing reconciliation breaks across US payment rails.
Rail, business date, settlement date, break type, ageing bucket, amount range, account, owner, resolution status.
Break dashboard, unmatched list, duplicate posting list, suspense ageing, owner work queue, rail-wise reconciliation status.
Accepted payments must reconcile to settlement, customer posting and GL; breaks create financial misstatement and customer-impact risk.
Triggered daily, at month-end, after incident recovery, during audit/exam, or when settlement-to-posting mismatch occurs.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
, Fedwire, ACH and internal payment data.
Match payment instruction, clearing/settlement record, core posting and GL entry. Flag if one or more legs are missing, duplicated, mismatched or aged beyond threshold.
Create common reconciliation mart, auto-match rules, ageing dashboards and workflow-based resolution.
Mandatory treasury/operations evidence where instant/wire payments fail or are constrained by liquidity/limits.
Daily / continuous monitoring; produced on request.
and Fedwire settlement require sufficient participant/master-account liquidity and effective limit controls.
Shows payments impacted by liquidity shortage, limit restrictions or threshold breaches.
Rail, business date, account, participant, limit type, liquidity threshold, breach flag, failed-payment flag, amount range.
trend, limit breach list, failed due to liquidity, top-up history, threshold alerts, payment-level drilldown.
Liquidity/limit failures can stop otherwise valid payments and create service reliability issues, especially for instant and wire rails.
Triggered intraday, after failed payment due to liquidity, treasury review, participant review, or limit threshold breach.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
The module must correlate payment failure/queuing with available balance, reserved balance and active limit configuration.
Calculate available liquidity as opening balance + inflows - outflows - reserved amount. Flag payments where amount exceeds available liquidity or configured limit.
Starting point for intraday liquidity calculation.
Shows actual capacity to process payments.
Add intraday liquidity forecasting, threshold alerting, and integration with treasury dashboard.
Payment rejection analytics and root-cause report
Mandatory evidence where payment operator/examiner asks for failed-payment explanation or data-quality review.
Payment operators and bank examiners can request evidence of rejection trends, root causes and remediation for operationally failed payments.
Explains why payments were rejected and whether issues were corrected/resubmitted.
Rail, date range, reject code, reject category, customer segment, channel, participant, amount range, resubmission status.
Top rejection reasons, reject trend, payment drilldown, resubmitted items, failed-after-retry list, product/channel defect analysis.
Rejection root causes reveal payment-data quality gaps, configuration issues, account problems, timing failures and system defects.
Triggered by high reject rates, release defects, customer escalation, scheme review, audit or operational quality governance.
Regulatory / scheme requirement for the reporting module
rail-specific rejection codes into common business categories while preserving original scheme reason codes.
Map reject codes to categories such as format, validation, account, limit, liquidity, timeout, duplicate, operating window or technical. Calculate count/value and resubmission rate.
Determines code set and business rules.
Add common reject taxonomy, auto-suggested remediation, and backlog
How it Supports the Reporting Catalogue
ECB payment statistics and National Central Bank payment-statistics collection framework
Used as basis for EU/SEPA payment statistics, volume/value and settlement-oriented reports.
EBA Instant Payments Regulation uniform reporting framework
Used as basis for EU credit-transfer charges, payment-account charges and rejected-transaction share reporting.
EPC SEPA Credit Transfer and SEPA Instant Credit Transfer scheme rulebooks
Used as basis for SCT, SCT Inst, execution timing, timeout/reservation release and R-transaction evidence.
DORA major ICT-related incident reporting framework
Used as basis for EU payment-impact and payment-service availability incident reports.
Service Operating Procedures and Operating Circular 8
availability, funds availability, status, returns, limits and operational evidence
Federal Reserve Banks - Fedwire Funds Service, Regulation J and Operating Circular 6
Used as basis for Fedwire message audit, operating-window, ISO 20022, settlement and reconciliation reports.
